rationale.claude: This is the editorial bar. Every section made decisive cuts: BEAT THE BOMB is 6 bullets (cap), 1-800-GOT-JUNK is 4 bullets (compressed appropriately for older role), 68W is just 2 bullets (heavily compressed — keeps only the training-curriculum + timed-assessment pieces that map to support-team transferable skills). Skills are GROUPED into 4 themed buckets ('Service Desk & Support', 'Systems & Networking', 'Tools & Documentation', 'Soft') instead of one untriaged blob — this is the structural move that distinguishes target from nearmiss. Summary opens with the JD title verbatim ('Service Desk Technician candidate'). Dates use canonical 'Sept 2024 - Mar 2026' (one consistent abbreviation, dash separator). Strategic Simulation project and Hospitality role were dropped entirely — decisions that demonstrate editorial confidence.

key_strengths: Skills GROUPED into 4 themed buckets — recruiter can scan each category independently
BTB section limited to exactly 6 bullets, each starting with a strong verb and scoped to one concern
68W military section compressed to 2 bullets — kept only the transferable-skills pieces, dropped the CBRN/decontamination scope that's noise for help-desk work
Summary opens with the JD title verbatim and ends with a concrete experience claim ('three years of technical operations work')
Old roles compressed appropriately — 1-800-GOT-JUNK has 4 bullets (down from 7) because it's >3 years stale; recent role gets the bullet budget
key_weaknesses: 'Sept' is non-canonical — convention is 3-letter 'Sep' (minor; this is the only date drift in the doc)
specific_fixes: Change 'Sept 2024 - Mar 2026' to 'Sep 2024 - Mar 2026' for canonical 3-letter month abbreviations
tier_definition_match: Matches target (~9.4): the editorial decisions are visible and confident. This is what the pipeline output should aspire to look like. Differences vs nearmiss are not gaps — they're CUTS that nearmiss didn't make (older role compression, themed skills grouping, military section trim, dropped projects/roles).
